place: Niagara Falls New York, New York

facts.text: Niagara Falls city sits on the Niagara River, directly opposite the Canadian city of Niagara Falls, sharing the famous waterfalls.
In 1927 the city annexed La Salle, a village named for explorer Robert de la Salle, expanding its borders.
2020 saw the opening of Cataract Commons, a public square on Old Falls Street that revitalized downtown.
The Niagara Falls Police Department, founded in 1892, employs 155 sworn officers, distinct from the smaller Town of Niagara force.
Hydroelectric power has shaped the city since 1895, when the first large‑scale plant opened, later becoming the site of the historic Schoellkopf Power Station.
The city hosts the Niagara Discovery Center and the Underground Railroad Heritage Center, preserving stories of the region’s past.
